bill.constitutionalAuthorityStatementText: [Congressional Record Volume 171, Number 113 (Monday, June 30, 2025)][House]From the Congressional Record Online through the Government Publishing Office [www.gpo.gov]By Mr. BARR:H.J. Res. 103.Congress has the power to enact this legislation pursuantto the following:Congress has the authority to propose amendments to theConstitution of the United States pursuant to Article V ofthe Constitution, which provides that Congress may, by a two-thirds vote of both the House of Representatives and theSenate, propose amendments to the Constitution.[Page H3037]

item.title: A joint resolution proposing an amendment to the Constitution of the United States to protect United States citizenship.

latestAction.text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary.

item.title: Proposing an amendment to the Constitution of the United States to protect United States citizenship.

latestAction.text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

item.text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

bill.title: Proposing an amendment to the Constitution to protect American citizenship.

item.title: Proposing an amendment to the Constitution to protect American citizenship.
Proposing an amendment to the Constitution to protect American citizenship.

latestAction.text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.
dublinCore.dc:contributor: Congressional Research Service, Library of Congress
dublinCore.dc:description: This file contains bill summaries and statuses for federal legislation. A bill summary describes the most significant provisions of a piece of legislation and details the effects the legislative text may have on current law and federal programs. Bill summaries are authored by the Congressional Research Service (CRS) of the Library of Congress. As stated in Public Law 91-510 (2 USC 166 (d)(6)), one of the duties of CRS is "to prepare summaries and digests of bills and resolutions of a public general nature introduced in the Senate or House of Representatives". For more information, refer to the User Guide that accompanies this file.
